errors: [The logic for "Has Osteoporotic Fracture" in the ELM does not match the CPG requirements. The CPG requires an exclusion based on a history of osteoporotic fractures (implying the presence of a diagnosis/condition), but the ELM implementation uses a `Not` operator on an `IsNull` check for `clinicalStatus`. This logic would incorrectly trigger an exclusion for any patient who has a fracture record with a clinical status present, rather than specifically checking if the record belongs to the "Osteoporotic Fractures" value set as intended by the query structure.]

[Minimum Age: ELM uses a literal value of 25 years, whereas the CPG requires 18 years. Encounter to Screen for Blood Pressure: ELM uses a look-back interval of 6 months, whereas the CPG requires 12 months (1 year).]
The "Age range" expression in the ELM implements an upper age limit of 80 years (`"value": "80"`), which contradicts the CPG requirement of 85 years or younger.
The HbA1c Laboratory Test logic in the ELM uses a threshold value of 9 (with unit "g%") instead of the required 6.5% specified in the Clinical Practice Guideline.

[The Rationale in the ELM implementation is truncated; it only includes the first sentence of the CPG requirement ("USPSTF recommends screening for cervical cancer every 3 years with cervical cytology alone in women aged 21-65 years.") and omits the second sentence regarding hrHPV testing for women aged 30-65 years.]

[The ELM implementation fails to include the "20 pack-year smoking history" requirement in the 'Has Smoking History' logic; it only checks for the presence of a 'Tobacco User' code in the Condition resource. Additionally, the 'Rationale' text in the ELM is a truncated version of the required Rationale text specified in the CPG.]

[The Rationale in the ELM implementation ("USPSTF recommends one-time AAA screening with ultrasonography in men aged 65-75 years who have ever smoked.") does not match the Rationale required by the CPG ("USPSTF recommends one-time screening for abdominal aortic aneurysm (AAA) with ultrasonography in men aged 65-75 years who have ever smoked (100+ cigarettes in lifetime). Grade B recommendation.")]

The Rationale in the ELM implementation ("USPSTF recommends screening for unhealthy alcohol use in primary care settings in adults 18 years or older.") does not match the Rationale required by the CPG ("USPSTF recommends screening for unhealthy alcohol use in primary care settings in adults 18 years or older, including pregnant women, and provide persons engaged in risky or hazardous drinking with brief behavioral counseling interventions. Grade B recommendation.").

[The 'Rationale' in the ELM implementation does not match the CPG requirement. The CPG requires: "USPSTF recommends that clinicians ask all adults about tobacco use, advise them to stop using tobacco, and provide behavioral interventions and U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA)-approved pharmacotherapy for cessation. Grade A recommendation." The ELM provides a truncated version: "USPSTF recommends that clinicians ask all adults 18+ about tobacco use and provide behavioral interventions for cessation."]

; The "Minimum Age" expression uses an age range of 60 to 75 years, whereas the CPG requires an age range of 45 to 75 years.; The "Has Recent Colorectal Screening" expression uses a lookback interval of 2 years, whereas the CPG requires a lookback interval of 1 year.; The "Rationale" expression returns `Null` regardless of the condition, whereas the CPG requires a specific text string when `InPopulation` is true.

[The 'Has Recent Anxiety Screening' expression uses a 6-month interval (Today minus 6 months) instead of the 1-year interval required by the CPG. Additionally, the 'Rationale' expression is incorrectly implemented; it returns 'Null' regardless of whether 'InPopulation' is true or false, whereas the CPG requires the specific USPSTF rationale text when screening is needed.]

; The age requirement in the ELM "In Demographic" expression is incorrectly set to `GreaterOrEqual` 30 years, whereas the CPG requires an age range of 15 to 65 years.; The "Rationale" expression in the ELM is incorrectly implemented; it returns `Null` for both the `then` and `else` branches, failing to provide the required rationale text specified in the CPG.

; In the "Is Obese" statement, the threshold value is set to 35 (kg/m2) instead of the required 25 (kg/m2) specified in the CPG.; In the "Has Recent Glucose Test" statement, the time interval is set to 1 year instead of the required 3 years specified in the CPG.; In the "Rationale" statement, the logic is inverted; it returns `Null` when `InPopulation` is true, whereas the CPG requires the rationale text to be provided when screening is needed.

[The 'In Demographic' expression uses a lower age bound of 30 years, whereas the CPG requirement specifies an age range of 21 to 65 years. Additionally, the 'Rationale' text in the ELM is truncated and does not match the full text provided in the CPG requirements.]

[The time interval for "Has Recent Adult Depression Screening" and "Has Recent Adolescent Depression Screening" is implemented as 3 months (Today minus 3 months), whereas the CPG requirement specifies a period of 1 year.]
[The 'In Demographic' expression uses a literal value of '75' for the age requirement, whereas the CPG requirement specifies '65' years or older.]

[The 'In Demographic' expression uses a literal value of '25' for age, whereas the CPG requirement specifies '18 years or older'. Additionally, the 'Rationale' text in the ELM implementation is truncated and does not match the full text provided in the CPG requirements.]
[The 'Has Recent DEXA Scan' expression uses a time interval of 6 months (Today minus 6 months) instead of the required 2 years specified in the CPG.]
[The 'In Demographic' expression uses a lower age bound of 55 years, whereas the CPG requirement specifies a minimum age of 65 years.]

The logical structure of `MeetsInclusionCriteria` in the ELM uses an `Or` operator for `Screening Age` and `Gender` (requiring only one of them to be true), whereas the CPG requirements specify that `ALL` of the following must be true (an `And` relationship) for both Age and Gender.

[The logic for "Connective Tissue Disorders (SNOMED)" (localId: 350) is inverted. The CPG requires excluding patients WITH a confirmed active Connective Tissue Disorder. However, the ELM implements this as `not (exists (ActiveCondition(Confirmed([Connective Tissue Disorders (SNOMED) VS]))))`. When this is used in the `MeetsExclusionCriteria` logic (localId: 538), which is `Connective Tissue Disorders (SNOMED) OR Amiodarones`, the double negation results in the CDS incorrectly including patients with CTD and excluding those without it.]

[The logic for "Needs AAA Screening" fails to include the "Is Male" requirement. While "Is Male" is defined in the ELM, it is not referenced in the "Needs AAA Screening" expression, meaning the CDS would incorrectly trigger for females aged 65-75 who have a smoking history and no prior screening.]

The ELM implementation fails to include the exclusion criterion for "Active diagnosis of Bipolar Disorder" (Value Set `2.16.840.1.113883.3.600.450`) within the `Needs Depression Screening` logic. While the statement `Has Bipolar Diagnosis` is defined in the ELM, it is never referenced in the final decision logic, meaning a patient with Bipolar Disorder would incorrectly trigger a screening recommendation.

The ELM implementation of the "Colonoscopy" logic uses a "Greater" operator (count > 0) instead of checking if the count is 0. According to the CPG, "MeetsInclusionCriteria" requires "Colonoscopy (count of procedures in past 1 month = 0)". The current ELM logic results in `InPopulation` being true only if the patient is age 50+ AND has had a colonoscopy in the last month, which is the inverse of the requirement.

The logical implementation of "Needs Falls Prevention Screening" is inverted. The CPG requires screening if the patient is age >= 65 AND has NOT had a falls risk assessment in the past year. However, the ELM expression for "Needs Falls Prevention Screening" uses an `And` operator to require both "In Demographic" AND "Has Recent Falls Assessment", which means it only triggers for patients who *have* had a recent assessment. Additionally, the "Has Recent Falls Assessment" logic is used as a positive requirement in the final decision logic rather than a negative one.

The logical implementation of the exclusion criteria for Bipolar and Depression diagnoses is incorrect. The CPG requires that the patient has NO active Bipolar diagnosis AND NO active Depression diagnosis (effectively: `NOT Bipolar AND NOT Depression`). However, the ELM implements this as `NOT (Bipolar AND Depression)`. Under the current ELM logic, a patient with an active Bipolar diagnosis but NO active Depression diagnosis would incorrectly meet the inclusion criteria and trigger a recommendation.

The logical structure for "MeetsExclusionCriteria" is implemented using an 'And' operator (localId: 537) instead of an 'Or' operator. According to the CPG, a patient should be excluded if they have a Connective Tissue Disorder OR are on active amiodarone therapy. The current ELM logic only excludes patients if they meet BOTH criteria simultaneously.
[The ValueSet ID for "Fall Risk Assessment" in the ELM `valueSets` section is `2.16.840.1.113883.600.564`, which does not match the CPG requirement of `2.16.840.1.113883.3.464.1003.118.12.1028`]

[The ValueSet ID for "Alcohol Use Screening" in the ELM `valueSets` section is `2.16.840.1.113883.3.526.3.1278`, which does not match the CPG requirement of `2.16.840.1.113883.3.600.1542`. Additionally, the Rationale text in the ELM implementation is truncated and does not match the full text required by the CPG.]
